evidence_excerpt: Six months after diagnosis, the patient was well; the pericardial effusion had disappeared and inflammatory parameters normalized, but she had evolution with chronic arthritis and continued anakinra and methotrexate.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Diffuse arthritis appeared after initial improvement and persisted as chronic arthritis; at six months she was well with normalized labs, but remained on anakinra and methotrexate.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Within 12–24 hours of starting anakinra, clinical condition improved; apyretic at 24 h, heart rate and respiratory rate decreased, CRP fell to 17 mg/L.
Discharged from ICU 1 day after starting anakinra; oxygen saturation 98% on ambient air and breathing improved.
Diffuse arthritis appeared in the next few days after initial improvement.
Subcutaneous methotrexate was started at 15 mg/week following the appearance of diffuse arthritis.
Two local injections of triamcinolone hexacetonide were performed during follow-up.
Six months after diagnosis, the patient was well; the pericardial effusion had disappeared and inflammatory parameters had normalized.

notes_on_uncertainty: Index time approximated to the point when pericarditis confirmed the SoJIA diagnosis and anakinra was initiated. Follow-up only reported to six months, so outcomes beyond 12 months are not available. Timing of triamcinolone injections is unspecified within the follow-up period.
